game_name: King's Quest VI: Heir Today, Gone Tomorrow

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles scattered across Isle of the Beast
danger_signals.meaning: Impassable danger zones; player cannot proceed without solving puzzles or obtaining specific items

danger_signals.visual_form: Antagonist character model and dialogue exchanges
danger_signals.meaning: Primary threat to protagonist's goal; represents political danger and forced marriage plot

danger_signals.visual_form: Isle of the Beast heavily forested setting with dense vegetation
danger_signals.meaning: Visual communication of hostile, dangerous territory requiring caution and puzzle-solving

danger_signals.visual_form: Hidden area with distinct visual styling
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate danger manifestation; optional confrontation with death itself as threat

safety_signals.meaning: Home base and safe zone; center of kingdom governance and information hub

safety_signals.visual_form: Character models with talk interaction available
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction points for gathering information, clues, and puzzle solutions

safety_signals.visual_form: Item display in toolbar inventory system
safety_signals.meaning: Accumulated tools and resources that provide safety through puzzle-solving capability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crown imagery; Arabian Nights aesthetic associated with Isle of the Crown
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vizier Abdul Alhazred represents usurping faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Isle of the Crown as seat of power; throne room and royal architecture

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Antagonist character model; dialogue reveals tyrannical intentions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Control over Isle of the Crown during Cassima's absence; implied administrative takeover
Hidden island area inhabited exclusively by druids; distinct cultural zone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vizier Abdul Alhazred occupies position of authority on Isle of the Crown
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vizier actively plotting forced marriage; kingdom operates under usurped governance

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Political crisis / threat of forced marriage
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Antagonist presence; Arabian Nights theming creates exotic but unstable political context
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs discuss Cassima's predicament; Alexander's quest objectives center on rescue mission

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Alexander's shipwreck on Isle of the Crown
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game opening establishes arrival at shores; transition from ship to island environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player begins in vulnerable state; must gather information and resources to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct visual themes per island (Arabian Nights, Alice in Wonderland, Classical mythology, Beauty and Beast)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Magic map required for inter-island travel; islands cannot be reached by normal walking

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Self-contained island kingdom with unique cultural aesthetics per region
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kingdom largely isolated from outside world; political crisis is internal affair

environmental_storytelling.example: Isle of the Crown features Arabian Nights theme communicating exotic, mystery-laden political intrigue; Isle of the Beast inspired by Beauty and Beast with heavy forestation and magical barriers suggesting danger and isolation
Multiple islands with distinct aesthetics (Alice in Wonderland, Classical mythology) communicate cultural diversity and political compartmentalization within kingdom
Isle of the Crown as center of kingdom with throne room and royal architecture establishes political power structure visually
Magical barriers scattered across Isle of the Beast communicate danger zones and puzzle-gated content; physical manifestation of threat
Druid island and Death encounter locations exist as hidden regions, communicating optional narrative content and hidden dangers beyond main quest

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Actively seeks Cassima; haunted by memories; navigates puzzle-solving through dialogue and item interaction
Plans forced marriage with Cassima; maintains control over Isle of the Crown; opposes Alexander's rescue attempt

entity_behavioral_tells.state: In danger; absent from active gameplay
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Object of rescue; revealed through narrative and Alexander's visions; communicates threat through her forced marriage plot
Provide dialogue clues; offer puzzle hints; communicate world-state through conversation

notes: King's Quest VI uses a 2D point-and-click adventure interface with icon-based toolbar (walk, look, action, talk) to communicate interaction possibilities. World-state is primarily communicated through narrative dialogue, distinct visual theming of islands, and NPC interactions rather than dynamic environmental changes. The game employs multiple endings based on player choices, suggesting branching world-state manifestations. The vizier antagonist represents political danger; his presence as usurping force during Cassima's absence creates primary conflict. Magical barriers and hidden areas communicate danger through environmental design. The 'Guidebook to the Land of the Green Isles' included with the game serves copy-protection function while reinforcing world-building through supplementary lore documentation.
